QF.id: XC Investment Products and Market Developments_21

question.text: SEBI's norms on Total Expense Ratio (TER) for mutual funds serve to:

answer.text: Maximize fund manager compensation for superior performance
Protect investors by capping the total costs that can be charged to a mutual fund scheme, ensuring cost-efficiency
Ensure all mutual fund schemes charge the same expenses
Eliminate all costs associated with mutual fund management

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TER caps limit the total expenses (management fees, administration costs, etc.) that can be charged to a mutual fund scheme. This protects investors from excessive cost extraction that erodes returns. **The Trap:** TER norms don't equalize expenses across schemes or eliminate costs entirely — they set maximum limits. Lower AUM typically attracts higher TER percentage (slab-based structure). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers TER in detail. Series X-A discusses cost impact on investment returns. The TER framework was significantly revised in 2018. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Lower TER directly translates to higher returns for investors — when recommending similar funds, factor in TER differences. Even a 0.5% TER difference compounded over 20 years significantly impacts the corpus.
